---
pagetitle: "John Severini"
description: "PhD Student at Georgetown University studying International Relations, Political Theory, and Political Methodology."
image: images/headshot.jpg
page-layout: custom
toc: false
---

::: {.hero-section}
![](images/banner.png){.hero-bg}

::: {.hero-overlay}
:::

::: {.hero-content}
I'm John Severini, a PhD student at Georgetown University's department of Government and a co-founder of Georgetown's [Initiative on Artificial Intelligence and Democratic Citizenship](https://aidc.georgetown.edu/). I'll also be a Hans J. Morgenthau Fellow at the University of Notre Dame for the 2026-27 academic year. My research primarily centers on technology, military effectiveness, and the relationship between the state and the private sector. In addition, I write about political methodology, specifically focusing on mixed methods integration and the growing role of AI as a tool in social science. My academic work has been published at *International Security*, *Security Studies*, and *Oxford Intersections: AI in Society*. I've also written essays and commentary for venues like *Lawfare*, *War on the Rocks*, and *Palladium Magazine*.
:::

:::
